# Black Holes

A black hole is a region of spacetime where gravity is so strong that nothing,
not even light, can escape. The boundary beyond which escape is impossible is
called the event horizon. Black holes form when massive stars collapse at the
end of their lives. The point of infinite density at the center is called the
singularity. In 2019 the Event Horizon Telescope produced the first image of a
black hole, in the galaxy M87.
